title: OSMM Business Context Object
description: Durable, foundational Context: who a business is, what it sells, who it competes with, and what marketing is trying to accomplish for it. Canonical schema for object_type 'business_context'. The osmm-business-context-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

business_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'BIZ-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

industry.description: Primary industry / sector, in plain language (not a taxonomy code).

business_type.description: Governed business-type vocabulary. Pick the single closest value.

founded.description: Year founded, as a string. Optional.

description.description: 2-3 neutral, factual sentences: what the company does, who it serves, how it's distinctive.

products_and_services.description: Key offerings the company actually sells (names/types, not brochure copy). A lightweight portfolio list; each offering is structured in depth by a Product Context Object.

value_proposition.description: The core 'why us' as a single synthesized claim.

target_customers.description: Plain descriptions of who the business serves. Structure lives in linked Persona/Audience objects.

revenue_model.description: How the business makes money (one phrase or sentence).

scale_context.description: Optional marketing-relevant scale signal; never a precise financial filing.

revenue_range.description: Order-of-magnitude band (e.g. '$50-250M', '$10B+'), not a precise figure.

revenue_mix_notes.description: Segment or channel mix, if it shapes marketing's mandate.

business_model_notes.description: Anything structurally distinctive about how the business operates. Optional.

market_position.description: Where they sit in the market: challenger, category leader, premium niche, etc.

competitive_differentiators.description: What makes them meaningfully different from competitors, grounded in evidence.

marketing_objectives.description: What marketing is actively trying to accomplish.

marketing_challenges.description: The structural or situational obstacles marketing faces.

strategic_priorities.description: The broader business priorities that shape marketing's mandate.

brand_tone_notes.description: Brief characterization of brand voice/tone if evident. Optional; full brand identity lives in the Brand Context Object.

key_markets.description: Geographies or segments that matter. Optional.

linked_brand_context.description: Id of the Brand Context Object for this business. Use 'BRC-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
